name: Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular (Reserva de la Biosfera Alto Bernesga)
region_intro: Jogging around La Pola De Gordón offers diverse terrain within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve in León, Spain. The region features varied landscapes, including ancient beech forests, mountainous areas with challenging ascents, and serene river valleys carved by the Bernesga River. Runners can explore a network of trails ranging from accessible forest paths to demanding mountain routes, providing options for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around La Pola De Gordón The most popular jogging route is Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular (Reserva de la Biosfera Alto Bernesga), a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This route leads through one of Spain's most beautiful beech forests, offering a picturesque and accessible run. Another top favourite among local runners is Cruz de Fontañán loop from Pola de Gordón, a difficult 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain, rewarding runners with panoramic views from Pico Fontañán. Local runners also love the Cueto San Mateo Summit loop from La Pola de Gordón, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Jogging around La Pola De Gordón is defined by ancient beech forests, rugged mountainous terrain, and lush river valleys. The extensive network of routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult runs, catering to various ability levels. The routes in La Pola De Gordón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 4 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore La Pola De Gordón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes around La Pola de Gordón?
A: The region offers a diverse range of terrain, from accessible paths through ancient beech forests like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt; to challenging mountainous areas within the Cantabrian Mountains. You'll find serene river valleys carved by the Bernesga River, rugged slopes, and impressive geological formations like the Los Calderones Gorge. This variety ensures options for all types of runners, from leisurely jogs to strenuous trail runs.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for all fitness levels in La Pola de Gordón?
A: Yes, La Pola de Gordón caters to various fitness levels. You can find easy, moderate, and difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052003964/faedo-de-cinera-trail-running-circular-reserva-de-la-biosfera-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, while routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Fontañán loop from Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueto San Mateo Summit loop from La Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt; offer more challenging ascents and descents for experienced trail runners.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in the area?
A: You'll encounter stunning natural beauty. Highlights include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, known for its vibrant autumn colors, and dramatic geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer panoramic views from peaks such as Cueto San Mateo and Pico Fontañán, and you might even spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/859306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de San Salvador&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many running routes are available around La Pola de Gordón?
A: There are over 15 dedicated running routes around La Pola de Gordón, with many more hiking and cycling trails that can also be adapted for jogging. This extensive network provides numerous options for exploring the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in La Pola de Gordón?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052003964/faedo-de-cinera-trail-running-circular-reserva-de-la-biosfera-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a beautiful beech forest. Other circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Fontañán loop from Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueto San Mateo Summit loop from La Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in La Pola de Gordón?
A: The running routes in La Pola de Gordón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty, the tranquility of the trails, and the diverse landscapes, particularly the ancient beech forests and rewarding mountain views. The low traffic on many routes also enhances the running experience.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in La Pola de Gordón?
A: La Pola de Gordón offers excellent running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly beautiful, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage, especially in the beech forests. Autumn, in particular, transforms the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt; into a spectacle of colors. Summer is also suitable, especially in the mornings or evenings, though some higher mountain trails might be more exposed to the sun. Winter running is possible, but some higher elevation routes may be affected by snow and ice.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options in the area?
A: Yes, for families looking for an easier and more picturesque run, the paths through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt; are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052003964/faedo-de-cinera-trail-running-circular-reserva-de-la-biosfera-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faedo de Ciñera – trail running circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that is relatively short and accessible, making it a great option for a leisurely family jog amidst stunning scenery.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in La Pola de Gordón?
A: Generally, the natural environment of La Pola de Gordón, being part of the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve, is welcoming to dogs. Many trails are suitable for running with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access to ensure the safety of wildlife and other trail users.

Q: How can I access the starting points of the running routes, particularly regarding parking and public transport?
A: Many routes, especially those starting from La Pola de Gordón itself or nearby villages like Ciñera and Santa Lucía de Gordón, typically have designated parking areas. For public transport, La Pola de Gordón is generally accessible by bus services connecting it to larger towns in León. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for specific routes and stops that align with your chosen running trail's starting point.

Q: Are there any long-distance running challenges or routes in the region?
A: For those seeking a significant challenge, the region is part of the 'Dragon de Gordón' initiative, which connects 17 villages via a 69 km route. While designed for long-distance exploration, it offers a substantial challenge for runners who wish to tackle it in stages, providing varied terrain and a deep immersion into the local landscape.

Q: What makes La Pola de Gordón a unique destination for running?
A: La Pola de Gordón's unique appeal lies in its location within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve, offering an authentic and diverse natural environment. The combination of ancient beech forests, rugged mountains, and serene river valleys, along with a network of well-maintained trails, provides a tranquil and immersive experience. The region's rich biodiversity and geological points of interest add an extra layer of discovery to your runs.
tips: Good area with hardly any difficulty parallel to the Ciñera stream and it is perfect to go with the little ones.
